macro_segments.title: Retraction in Sales: Partial Payment and Lower Hand Doctrine

micro_segments.title: Overview of the daf and the mishnah case of retraction
Lower hand doctrine: rights of the non-retracting party to choose payment or land
Gemara's question: from whose property does the buyer collect?
Kasalka da'atach: buyer can choose from all seller's properties, not just the plot
Creditor comparison and why buyer should not get better treatment than lender
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's resolution: collection is only from within the plot being sold
Rav Acha's alternative: buyer can take best property if seller is pursuing funds

macro_segments.title: Finalization of Sale with Partial Payment: Intent and Circumstantial Evidence

micro_segments.title: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's teaching: finalizing sale with partial payment via written document
Baraita on eravon (collateral): conditions for forfeiture and matching obligations
Rebbi Yose's position: asmakhta kinyan—conditional agreements are binding
Rebbi Yehuda: eravon creates partial purchase based on collateral amount
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's distinction: explicit stipulation required for full purchase effect
Mishna case: without stipulation, partial payment alone finalizes full purchase

macro_segments.title: Contextual Resolution: Seller's Intent Based on Financial Circumstances

micro_segments.title: Gemara's reconciliation: seller running around for money versus not running around
Ayil venafik azuzei: evidence of urgent money need from seller's behavior
Rava's rule: seller pursuing funds means partial payment does not finalize

macro_segments.title: Partial Payment for Chattel and the Extension of Intent Doctrine

micro_segments.title: Case of partial payment on donkey sale with one zuz remaining
Ruling: any unpaid balance prevents finalization, even one zuz
Tosafot's reading: even for chattel, possession without full payment is not final if seller needs funds
Radical implication: disrupts assumption that possession finalizes chattel purchases

macro_segments.title: Contextual Resolution: Seller's Intent Based on Financial Circumstances (continued)

micro_segments.title: Counterbalancing factor: seller offloading unwanted property despite needing money
Complexity of contextual proof: debating seller's actual intent and desperation

macro_segments.title: Employment Law: Worker Breach and Employer Remedies in Davar Ha'aved Situations

micro_segments.title: Mishna case: worker backs out during davar ha'aved (spoilable work)
Employer remedies: deception of higher pay or hiring replacements at worker's cost
Limitation on hiring others: up to the amount of wages already earned by breaching worker
Rabah's refinement: if worker has already been paid, no offset available
Baraita distinction: offset applies only when worker deposited goods at workplace

macro_segments.title: Rental Terms and Breach: Negligence, Causation, and Changed Warranties

micro_segments.title: Mishna case: donkey rented for mountain use taken to valley instead
Negligence-linked liability: renter liable only if breach connects to damage cause
Slipping versus overheating: mountain terms affect slip risk; valley terms affect heat risk
Gemara debate: does violation of terms automatically void warranty, or require proof of causation?
Apparent solution: reisha represents automatic liability; seifa requires proven causation link
Air quality and weariness: grounds for liability even when causation is unclear
Renewed debate: reisha as Rebbi Meir (violation = gazlan status); seifa requires actual negligence

macro_segments.title: Liability for Breach of Rental Terms: The Gazlan Doctrine and Its Application

micro_segments.title: Rebbi Meir's principle: one who violates owner's intent becomes gazlan with full liability
Dyer case: if you dye wool wrong color, you've acquired it; pay its value
Purim funds case: if funds collected for Purim, no discretion to use otherwise
Poor man's shirt: giver stipulates shirt, not coat; breach of stipulation violates owner intent
Gemara confirmation: violation of stipulation shows Rebbi Meir's concern for owner intent
Unproven step: whether violation of terms automatically makes renter full gazlan liable

topical_tags.term: Ayil venafik azuzei (running around for money)
Mocher sadeh mipnei ra'asah (selling property one regrets)
Tchilaso b'pshiah v'sofo b'ones (begins with negligence ends with accident)
